title: quercus semecarpifolia var. spinosa
intro: Quercus semecarpifolia var. spinosa, also known as the spiny oak, is a plant species that belongs to the Quercus genus of deciduous trees, characterized by its thorny branches and edible acorns. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus semecarpifolia var. spinosa include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as supporting digestive health and immune system function. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies for various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and infections.
